items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: Mountain View is planning infrastructure and facility upgrades for the next fiscal year. Residents should watch the approved projects list to see what improvements are coming to parks, roads, and public facilities in their neighborhoods.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code that governs the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and protections in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan Gets Status Update
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on a comprehensive plan to improve San José library facilities and customer experience. This plan may signal upcoming changes to library services and building upgrades across the system.

items.headline: Downtown building could get bonus floor space for historic preservation
items.summary: A property at 405 Kipling Street is seeking to upgrade its historic designation from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of development floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed near San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two parcels on San Antonio Road into an 8-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents a significant density increase for that area and will go through the public hearing process.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled for an unknown date. Residents who were planning to weigh in on this project should wait for a new notice.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should monitor future updates for details on what infrastructure or project this contract covers.

items.headline: Grant Awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will support cultural programming and arts education in the community.
